checks.a_analysis: The output JSON accurately reflects the text found in the source document. No fabricated paragraphs or sentences were identified. Headings and checklist items match the source text exactly. Some metadata fields (like tenant_id) are part of the output schema but do not constitute fabricated document content.
There is no contact information (phone numbers, emails, or URLs) present in the source PDF, and none has been fabricated in the output JSON.

The source document consists of two pages. Both pages are represented in the output JSON across two main sections. All functional sections (Staffing & Operations Management, Documentation & Record Keeping, Technical Support & Infrastructure, and Emergency Operations Coordination) are present.
The source document contains no tables. The checklist items are correctly structured as lists in the JSON output.

checks.check_name: Significant text omission within a section
checks.a_analysis: All text content from the source checklist is present in the output. Every checklist item from all four sections has been captured accurately. The initial form fields (Year, Organization, Form Contact) are also included. Minor footer elements (page numbers and update date) are excluded as per acceptable metadata handling, which does not constitute a significant text omission.

checks.check_name: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
checks.a_analysis: The text in the JSON output matches the source document faithfully. This includes preservation of the source's misspelling of 'milage' and phrasing such as 'considering of an external resource request'. No changes in meaning or numerical data were found.

checks.a_analysis: The source document repeats the header information (Committee name and document title) on the second page. The output JSON faithfully represents this by including the header elements in both sections corresponding to the two pages. No unintended duplication of text blocks or list items was observed.

The JSON uses heading level 2 for the major committee name on each page. Subsection titles are defined within the section structure, which semantically places them below the main heading. The overall structure (Title -> Section Headings -> Subsection Titles) is logical and follows a standard hierarchy. No skips or incorrect nesting were found.
The source document does not contain any tables, and neither does the JSON output. This check is not applicable, resulting in a pass.

The reading order in the JSON perfectly matches the logical flow of the source PDF. It proceeds from the header and form fields on page 1 into the checklist sections, then repeats the header for page 2 followed by the final coordination section. Content within each page is ordered top-to-bottom as expected.

The checkbox items in the source are correctly converted into unordered list structures in the JSON. The number of items in each list (3, 3, 2 on page 1; 4 on page 2) matches the source document exactly. The meaning and nesting of these tasks are preserved.

checks.check_name: Heading count mismatch between JSON and HTML
checks.a_analysis: There is a mismatch between the explicit heading count in the JSON (2) and the rendered HTML (7). However, this is explained by the renderer promoting the document title (1) and subsection titles (4) to semantic headings for accessibility. Total headings = 1 (title) + 2 (explicit) + 4 (subsections) = 7. This is an expected normalization rather than a structural defect.

The JSON contains 0 links, matching the source text. The 1 link in the rendered HTML is likely a metadata link added to the pipeline's footer (an acceptable change) or a link associated with the QR code. No malicious or broken links were injected into the document content.

checks.a_analysis: The HTML output successfully maintains the visual organization of the source PDF. It converts the two pages into a single continuous document while preserving the thematic grouping of the checklist items. Although checkboxes are replaced with bullet points, the logical flow and hierarchy of sections (Staffing & Operations, Documentation, Technical Support, etc.) are consistent with the source. The multi-page header is repeated at the start of each corresponding section in the HTML, which faithfully represents the original layout.
There are no tables present in either the source PDF or the converted HTML output.
All meaningful images from the source PDF, including the 'Hurri Up' logo, the 'LGC' logo, and the QR codes at the bottom of each page, are present and correctly rendered in the HTML output.

The header text content ('North Carolina Local Government Committee' and 'Hurricane Preparedness Checklist During Event Tasks') is visually duplicated at the start of both page content blocks. It appears first as part of the header graphic container and then immediately repeats as a plain-text heading block directly below the graphic. This redundant repetition is not present in the source layout, where the text appears only once within the header area of each page.
checks.b_evidence_source: The text 'North Carolina Local Government Committee / Hurricane Preparedness Checklist / During Event Tasks' appears exactly once at the top of Page 1 and Page 2.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML screenshot shows this text sequence once within the graphical header box and then again as a series of bold headings immediately below the box at the start of each page section.
checks.b_evidence_location: Start of Page 1 and Page 2 content sections in HTML

checks.a_analysis: The visual quality of the form labels at the beginning of the checklist is degraded. In the source PDF, 'Year:', 'Organization:', and 'Form Contact:' are formatted as distinct vertical labels with associated input lines. In the HTML output, these labels are condensed into a single run-on block of text ('Year: Organization: Form Contact:'), losing the visual clarity and separation required for form fields.
checks.b_evidence_source: Page 1 shows 'Year:', 'Organization:', and 'Form Contact:' as three separate vertical fields on separate lines.
checks.b_evidence_output: The HTML rendering displays 'Year: Organization: Form Contact:' condensed into a single horizontal block of text.
checks.b_evidence_location: Immediately following the repeated header text at the start of Page 1
